goal: Orient on the fresh norfolk-path repo, then grill + plan the first Godot build slice and prep PROJECT.md for handoff to a separate build session.
completed: Oriented: repo was a blank dos-new *Python* CLI scaffold, not a Godot project as the spec requires
Ran /grill and resolved 7 design decisions for the game's first slice, one at a time
Wrote the approved Slice 1 'walking skeleton' plan and committed it into the repo as docs/slice-1-plan.md
Filled in PROJECT.md: real identity, v1 scope, Locations, Godot 4.7 command block
Committed and pushed both to branch claude/next-steps-repo-overview-iiyh7z
discovered: The scaffold (typer/pydantic, uv/pytest/ruff, Python-matrix CI) fundamentally mismatches a Godot 4 game; the global CLAUDE.md 'standard scaffold' does not apply here
Godot projects are text files (project.godot/.tscn/.gd), so a cloud agent can author the whole project headless without the editor GUI
Movement 'feel' cannot be verified headless - it is a human-in-editor task on Windows; only runs/exports are agent-verifiable
The session-local plan file under /root/.claude/plans does not travel to a separate clone, so the plan had to be committed into the repo for the build session to see it
decisions: Rip out the Python scaffold entirely; clean Godot project at repo root; keep .claude/, CLAUDE.md, PROJECT.md, session hook
Pin Godot 4.7 stable (June 2026) on both the cloud build and the Windows playtest ends

Slice 1 = walking skeleton: one player, free 8-directional movement with accel/friction weight, placeholder TileMapLayer level, target-agnostic follow camera (3x zoom, 640x360), both input maps defined but only P1 spawned
Prove the HTML5 Web export in Slice 1 to de-risk the browser pipeline early; Step 0 = install Godot 4.7 headless + prove a trivial export BEFORE authoring the project
Build/verify split: build session authors files + headless sanity-check; Chris pulls to Windows and owns movement-feel tuning via exported constants
lessons: When handing off to a separate cloud session, commit the plan INTO the repo - the session-local plan file does not travel to a fresh clone
On a scaffold/domain mismatch, make it the very first grill question: it is the root dependency that project structure, CI, and commands all hang off
Spike the riskiest unknown (toolchain install + export) before authoring a full slice, not after
open_questions: Will Godot 4.7 headless + Web export templates install cleanly in the cloud env through the agent proxy? (the Step 0 spike answers this)
Web threads-on (needs COOP/COEP serve headers) vs threads-off for Slice 1 - to be decided during the build
Which friends are allies vs comic villains, and the actual quest/story beats - still unwritten (v1, not Slice 1)
Hours/week is still unsized for pacing expectations
intent: This was the planning/handoff session; the actual Godot build is deliberately deferred to a separate session (Vela). Ended once the repo was prepped (PROJECT.md + committed plan) and pushed so Vela picks up cleanly on a fresh clone.
next_task: Kick off Vela on branch claude/next-steps-repo-overview-iiyh7z to build Slice 1 per docs/slice-1-plan.md, starting with the Step 0 Godot 4.7 toolchain spike.
dont_retry: Keeping the Python scaffold or its uv/pytest/ruff CI for this Godot project
Authoring the full slice before proving the Godot install + Web export works (Step 0 must come first)
